name: Turkey Cotolette Alla Romagnola
recipeIngredient: 4 turkey cutlets, each about 1/2 inch thick (approximately 1 1/2 lbs total)
1/4 pound Parmigiano-Reggiano, shaved into shards with a peeler
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the turkey pieces with salt and pepper.
2. Dip each piece in the egg mixture, coating evenly.
3. Dredge the coated pieces in bread crumbs, pressing to ensure even coverage.
4. Place on a plate and refrigerate for 1 hour.
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
6. In a saute pan (14 to 16 inches), heat olive oil and butter over medium heat until foam dissipates.
7. Carefully place the turkey pieces in the pan, cooking slowly until golden brown on one side, about 6 to 7 minutes. Do not rush this step; otherwise, they may burn.
8. Turn the pieces and cook the other side until both sides are golden brown.
9. Pour tomato sauce and chicken stock around the meat, bringing to a boil.
10. Place a slice of Prosciutto over each piece and sprinkle with shaved Parmigiano-Reggiano.
11. Cover the pan and place in the oven for 10 minutes.
12. Remove from the oven and transfer to a warmed serving platter.
13. Drizzle with spoonfuls of sauce and serve.
Yield: 4 servings
